title: Green Bean Bredie
excerpt: Ingredients 1,5 kg stewing meat lamb or beef e.g. brisket, lamb shanks, chuck etc. 2 onions sliced into rings 1 tinned tomato 10 ml cumin (optional) 5 cloves 250 ml white wine 300 ml warm water 100 ml Worcestershire sauce 60 ml vinegar salt and pepper to taste 3 potatoes cut into cubes 1 …
html:
Ingredients

  • 1,5 kg stewing meat lamb or beef e.g. brisket, lamb shanks, chuck etc.
  • 2 onions sliced into rings
  • 1 tinned tomato
  • 10 ml cumin (optional)
  • 5 cloves
  • 250 ml white wine
  • 300 ml warm water
  • 100 ml Worcestershire sauce
  • 60 ml vinegar
  • salt and pepper to taste
  • 3 potatoes cut into cubes
  • 1 kg green beans
  • a pinch of nutmeg ("nutmeg")

Method

You can take a shortcut here and quickly steam your meat a little in the steamer for about 25 to 30 minutes.

Fry the onions in oil until glassy.

Fry the cumin together with the onions.

Cut the meat into smaller cubes and fry together with the Worcestershire sauce and cloves.

Let it cook for about 60 minutes – if your sauce gets low, add more water. And if it's too thin, thicken with a little cornflour (Maizena).

Now add the potatoes, cut into quarters, and cook for another 20 minutes.

Lastly, add the sliced beans, salt and pepper.

Cook now until everything is tender, but not mushy.

Original recipe – By: Marindakook

Easy!
